title: InstCombine: Implement SimplifyDemandedFPClass for fma
summary:
The developers generalized the optimization by abstracting operand analysis from binary to N-ary operations. They replaced `inferFastMathValueFlagsBinOp` (hardcoded for two operands) with `inferFastMathValueFlags`, which uses an array of `KnownFPClass` to handle any number of inputs. This allowed the same inference logic to be reused for ternary operations like `llvm.fma` and `llvm.fmuladd`.

They added a dedicated case for these intrinsics in `SimplifyDemandedUseFPClass`, computing result classes via `KnownFPClass::fma`/`fma_square`. The transformation now propagates `nofpclass` constraints from all operands to the result, inferring `noNaNs`/`noInfs` fast-math flags when proven. This pattern demonstrates how to extend operand-specific analyses to support variadic operations systematically.
